03/06/2026 22:50:12   Most compositing window managers like 'compiz' or 'beryl'
03/06/2026 22:50:12   cause X DAMAGE to fail, and so you may not see any screen
03/06/2026 22:50:12   updates via VNC.  Either disable 'compiz' (recommended) or
03/06/2026 22:50:12   supply the x11vnc '-noxdamage' command line option.
03/06/2026 22:50:12 
03/06/2026 22:50:12 Wireframing: -wireframe mode is in effect for window moves.
03/06/2026 22:50:12   If this yields undesired behavior (poor response, painting
03/06/2026 22:50:12   errors, etc) it may be disabled:
03/06/2026 22:50:12    - use '-nowf' to disable wireframing completely.
03/06/2026 22:50:12    - use '-nowcr' to disable the Copy Rectangle after the
03/06/2026 22:50:12      moved window is released in the new position.
03/06/2026 22:50:12   Also see the -help entry for tuning parameters.
03/06/2026 22:50:12   You can press 3 Alt_L's (Left "Alt" key) in a row to 
03/06/2026 22:50:12   repaint the screen, also see the -fixscreen option for
03/06/2026 22:50:12   periodic repaints.
03/06/2026 22:50:12 
03/06/2026 22:50:12 XFIXES available on display, resetting cursor mode
03/06/2026 22:50:12   to: '-cursor most'.
03/06/2026 22:50:12   to disable this behavior use: '-cursor arrow'
03/06/2026 22:50:12   or '-noxfixes'.
03/06/2026 22:50:12 using XFIXES for cursor drawing.
03/06/2026 22:50:12 GrabServer control via XTEST.
03/06/2026 22:50:12

03/06/2026 22:50:12   If this yields undesired behavior (poor response, painting

******************************************************************************
Have you tried the x11vnc '-ncache' VNC client-side pixel caching feature yet?

The scheme stores pixel data offscreen on the VNC viewer side for faster
retrieval.  It should work with any VNC viewer.  Try it by running:

    x11vnc -ncache 10 ...

One can also add -ncache_cr for smooth 'copyrect' window motion.
More info: http://www.karlrunge.com/x11vnc/faq.html#faq-client-caching
